section.para: This report has prosecuted a systematic, evidence-based indictment of five foundational ideologies of the modern era. The analysis demonstrates that Kantian ethics, Humanism, egotism, libertarianism, and Transhumanism are not discrete philosophies but form an interconnected and coherent intellectual system. This system is predicated on a shared, demonstrably false conception of the human: an autonomous, rational, self-sufficient, and able-bodied individual. This normative subject is a legal fiction, a biological fantasy, and a psychological fallacy.

The lineage of harm is clear. Kantianism provides the abstract, rational subject, divorced from the realities of embodied, emotional, and interdependent life. Humanism takes this abstract subject and makes it the universal measure of all things, enshrining a productivist and ableist norm that devalues those who do not conform and justifies the exploitation of the non-human world. Egotism and libertarianism weaponize this subject's fictional autonomy against the collective, promoting social structures that are scientifically incompatible with the biological imperative of interdependence and legally negligent in their disregard for non-consensual harm. Finally, Transhumanism represents the terrifying apotheosis of this entire tradition. It seeks to take the normative subject of Humanism, empower it with the radical individualism of libertarianism, and use technology to perfect it into a new, posthuman god, casting aside "normal" humanity as a flawed prototype in the process.

The absolute condemnation of this entire lineage is therefore a necessary act of intellectual and ethical hygiene. It is the essential precondition for constructing social, legal, and scientific realities based on the empirical truths of interdependence, malleability, and relationality. The central task for any project of genuine human—and more-than-human—flourishing is the dismantling of the normative cage. The ideologies that built and continue to reinforce that cage must be unequivocally rejected, not on the basis of sentiment or competing moral claims, but on the firm ground of legal principle and scientific fact.

row.entry: Moral worth is derived from rational autonomy.
Human cognition is not purely rational but is shaped by emotion, biology, and environment.6
Its exclusionary definition of "humanity" provides a philosophical basis for legal frameworks that deny capacity to the "irrational".2
Human flourishing is achieved through reason and control over nature.
The "myth of independence" and productivism are ecologically catastrophic and biologically unsustainable.2
Its anthropocentrism enables the legal classification of nature as property, and its productivism is enshrined in norms like UDHR Art. 23, which devalues non-productive persons.2
The individual is sovereign and absolute liberty is the highest good.
The biological reality of human existence is interdependence, from the microbial (gut-brain axis) to the societal (care networks).2
The concept of absolute procreative liberty constitutes culpable negligence and the non-consensual imposition of harm, violating a duty of care.11
A moral right exists to use technology for radical self-enhancement.
Ignores the social psychology of stratification, which predicts that enhancement technologies will inevitably create new, severe social hierarchies.1
Directly violates legally binding international bioethics law (Oviedo Convention, Art. 13) and the foundational principles of equality and inherent dignity in the UDHR (Art. 1 & 2).1

listitem.para: A Bio-Existential Framework for Systemic Improvement: Analysis and Application
Limits of Mind and Behavior Control
Designing a Better Doll Collar Complex
Project Chimera: A Longitudinal, Participatory, and Autoethnographic Study of Symbiotic Integration and the Co-Creation of an Interstitial Self
